name: SFE Canonical Question Schema
description: Universal template. Every concept must answer all 6 question classes before it can be promoted to Canon. Questions are contracts, not prompts.
principle: You cannot claim a concept exists unless you can answer all 6 with citations. If the schema cannot be satisfied, return REFUSE_INSUFFICIENT_GROUNDING plus gaps.

question_classes.name: Operational Identity
question_classes.canonical_phrasing: What is this concept operationally, not poetically?
phrasing_variants: What does this concept DO in this domain?
If this concept were absent, what would change?
What is the minimum definition that preserves predictive power?
What is this NOT, to distinguish it from adjacent concepts?

answer_schema.fail_closed_condition: If operational_definition is circular or contains the concept term itself without decomposition

failure_triggers: Cannot distinguish from at least one adjacent concept
Definition varies by >0.5 across domains without domain variant declared

question_classes.name: Measurement Operationalization
question_classes.canonical_phrasing: How do we observe or measure this concept in this domain?
phrasing_variants: What is the measurement procedure?
What observable signals indicate this concept is present?
What is the measurement schema and its required fields?
What precision is required for this measurement to be useful?
What are the measurement failure modes?

answer_schema.fail_closed_condition: If concept cannot be observed or measured by any method, even indirect proxy

question_classes.name: Scope and Boundary Conditions
question_classes.canonical_phrasing: Where does this concept apply, and where does it fail?
phrasing_variants: Under what conditions does this concept hold?
What are the boundary conditions that invalidate this concept?
What is the scope of valid application?
What conditions cause concept failure or mismatch?
What are the edge cases that stress the definition?

answer_schema.fail_closed_condition: If valid_scope is unbounded (concept claimed to apply everywhere)

failure_triggers: Concept applied outside declared scope without domain_variant
Edge cases not enumerated for high-stakes domain

question_classes.name: Decision Dependence
question_classes.canonical_phrasing: What actions change if this concept is true vs false?
phrasing_variants: What decisions are downstream of this concept?
What would we do differently if this concept did not exist?
What is the counterfactual decision tree?
Which actions require this concept to be resolved before proceeding?
What is the decision stake of this concept?

required_fields.description: How much does a change in this concept change downstream action?
Are any irreversible actions gated on this concept?

answer_schema.fail_closed_condition: If no dependent decisions can be identified — concept may be decorative, not functional
answer_schema.high_stakes_rule: If irreversible_actions_gated is true, allowed_uncertainty drops to 0.15

failure_triggers: Decision sensitivity below threshold for deployment domain
Irreversible action gated on concept with confidence < 0.85

question_classes.name: Falsification Conditions
question_classes.canonical_phrasing: What outcome pattern would prove we are using this concept wrong?
phrasing_variants: What evidence would force a concept revision?
What prediction does this concept make that could fail?
What outcome would trigger a major version change?
What is the falsification test for this concept?
What does systematic failure of this concept look like?

required_fields.description: What this concept predicts about outcomes
How we would test this concept in practice

answer_schema.fail_closed_condition: If no falsification condition can be specified — concept is unfalsifiable and cannot be promoted to Canon

failure_triggers: Concept promoted to Canon without falsification test
Known failures not incorporated into boundary updates

question_classes.name: Cross-Domain Translation
question_classes.canonical_phrasing: What is preserved and what is lost when mapping this concept to another domain?
phrasing_variants: Is this the same concept as [X] in domain [Y]?
What is the translation loss between domain A and domain B?
Which basis questions survive the domain transfer?
What assumptions must change for this concept to work in domain Y?
Is this concept transferable or domain-locked?

required_fields.description: True if concept cannot transfer without major loss
True structural isomorphisms to other domains

answer_schema.fail_closed_condition: If transfer claimed with translation_loss=0 but lost_questions is non-empty

failure_triggers: Isomorphism claimed without shared basis questions

refuse_grounding_response.principle: A refused grounding is not a failure. It is the system working correctly. It prevents semantic drift before it starts.

completeness_gate.description: A concept cannot be promoted to Canon unless all 6 question classes have validated answers with evidence.
completeness_gate.partial_deployment: Concepts with completeness_score >= 0.5 can be used as EXPERIMENTAL. >= 0.8 as PROPOSED. 1.0 required for CANONICAL.
